_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Develop detailed security design documentation for component and interface specifications to support system design and development.
reference_document_element_description: Develop and maintain operational documentation for data systems, including standard operating procedures, runbooks, escalation matrices, and knowledge base articles.
comments: DCWF task intersects with WIDAI OP domain. OP-T-010 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: AI-T-006 (AI, cosine: 0.505), AI-T-002 (AI, cosine: 0.496).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 6.2/10 (raw 0.617). Shared vocabulary: [develop, documentation]. DCWF-only terms [component, design, detailed, development, interface, security] vs WIDAI-only terms [articles, base, data, escalation, knowledge, maintain] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.874 (P=0.886, R=0.862). P exceeds R by 0.024 — the DCWF element's 16 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 19 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the DCWF element.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: contradiction-dominant (100.0%). Despite 'Intersects with' classification, the model detects semantic tension between "Develop detailed security design documentation for component and interface speci" and "Develop and maintain operational documentation for data systems, including stand". Indicates these concepts operate in adjacent but distinct professional domains — shared vocabulary, different application context.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.507 < STS 0.617 (gap -0.110). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [component, design, detailed] and [articles, base, data] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 14.3%. Shared tokens: [develop, documentation]. DCWF-only: [component, design, detailed, development, interface, security]. WIDAI-only: [articles, base, data, escalation, knowledge, maintain].

signals.detail: NLI contradiction 100.0% suggests semantic tension between the FDE and matched KSA. The competency domains may be adjacent but not functionally aligned.
